name: gbd-mapping

metadata.name: gbd-mapping
metadata.summary: A programmatically accessible mapping of gbd entities.

documentation:
# GBD Mapping

```{image} https://badge.fury.io/py/gbd-mapping.svg
:target: https://badge.fury.io/py/gbd-mapping
```

```{image} https://github.com/ihmeuw/gbd_mapping/actions/workflows/build.yml/badge.svg?branch=main
:alt: Latest Version
:target: https://github.com/ihmeuw/gbd_mapping
```

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/gbd_mapping/badge/?version=latest
:alt: Latest Docs
:target: https://gbd_mapping.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest
```

Mapping of Global Burden of Disease (GBD) entities to their metadata.

There are two packages offered in this distribution. The first, the `gbd_mapping_generator`
is a set of scripts that define templates and data gathering code used to produce the second, the `gbd_mapping`.
The `gbd_mapping_generator` package will not function without access to the IHME cluster and some of our
internally used data access libraries. Mapping updates are managed by an automated toolchain, so this shouldn't
be an issue.

The `gbd_mapping` is a programmatically accessible (and TAB-complete-able) set of mappings for GBD entities
including:

> - Causes
> - Risks
> - Covariates
> - Etiologies
> - Sequelae

You can install `gbd_mapping` from PyPI with pip:

> `> pip install gbd_mapping`

or build it from source with

> `> git clone https://github.com/ihmeuw/gbd_mapping.git`
>
> `> cd gbd_mapping`
>
> `> pip install .`

## Development and Mapping Generation

In order to generate or regenerate the mappings from data, you must have access to
the Institute for Health Metrics and Evaluation cluster and internal PyPI server.
Contact \<<mailto:collijk@uw.edu>> if you need further instructions on that.

Given proper permissions, you can set up this library in development mode with

> `> git clone https://github.com/ihmeuw/gbd_mapping.git`
>
> `> cd gbd_mapping`
>
> `> pip install -e .['dev']`

### [Check out the docs!](https://vivarium.readthedocs.io/projects/gbd-mapping/en/latest/)

modules.name: gbd_mapping_generator.cause_builder

functions.name: gbd_mapping_generator.covariate_builder.make_covariate
documentation:
Creates a single Covariate based on the supplied parameters.

Parameters
----------
name
    Covariate name.
cov_id
    The numeric id for the covariate.
by_age
    Adjusted by age
by_sex
    Adjusted by sex
dichotomous
    Is dichotomous

Returns
-------
str
    Generated string for a single covariate.

functions.name: gbd_mapping_generator.covariate_builder.make_covariates
documentation:
Generates the list of covariates.

Parameters
----------
covariate_list
    Sequence of covariate data from which to generate the representation

Returns
-------
str
    Generated string all covariates.

functions.name: gbd_mapping_generator.util.make_module_docstring
documentation:
Generates standard header with additional information from the description.

Parameters
----------
description
    Custom header text for the generated file.
file
    Path to python module that generates the target module.

Returns
-------
str
    String representation of module doc string.

functions.name: gbd_mapping_generator.util.make_import
documentation:
Generates the necessary imports. Smart about importing modules or names.

Parameters
----------
module_to_import
    Name of the module.
imports
    Named items to import. If empty import the module name.

Returns
-------
str
    Generated string for necessary imports.

functions.name: gbd_mapping_generator.util.replace_numeric_prefix
documentation:
Variables cannot start with numeric characters. Replace with the word.
This only occurs 3 times using GBD 2019 data.

Parameters
----------
name
    The name of the member variable.

Returns
-------
str
    Input string transformed to non-numeric prefix.

functions.name: gbd_mapping_generator.util.to_id
documentation:
Wrap the id value with the id type. Be mindful of NaN values.

Parameters
----------
number
    The id value.
id_type
    The id type.

Returns
-------
str
    String with the id type wrapping the value in parentheses.

functions.name: gbd_mapping_generator.util.make_class_sig
documentation:
Generate class signature from a name and additional information.

Parameters
----------
name
    Name of the generated class.
superclass
    Parent class.
docstring
    Documentation for the generated class.

Returns
-------
str
    String representation of named class.

functions.name: gbd_mapping_generator.util.make_slots
documentation:
Generate explicit object attributes using slots (instead of dict).

Parameters
----------
field_list
    Names for the slot attributes.

Returns
-------
str
    String representation of slot attributes.

functions.name: gbd_mapping_generator.util.make_init
documentation:
Generate the __init__ function as part of class generation.

Parameters
----------
field_dtype_tuples
    Collection of name / value pairs that are used to create the
    the __init__ function with the specified parameters. Parameters
    are type hinted.

Returns
-------
str
    String representation of the __init__ function.

functions.name: gbd_mapping_generator.util.make_super_call
documentation:
Generate the call to initialize the parent class.

Parameters
----------
superclass
    Nested tuples composed of name and attributes for the superclass.

Returns
-------
str
    String representation of the call to the superclass.

functions.name: gbd_mapping_generator.util.make_attribute_assignment
documentation:
Generate the class attributes and initialize them.

Parameters
----------
field_names
    List of class attribute names.

Returns
-------
str
    String representation of the initialized attributes.

functions.name: gbd_mapping_generator.util.make_record
documentation:
Generate class definitions from a name and additional information.

Parameters
----------
name
    Name of the generated class.
attrs
    Class attributes.
superclass
    Parent class.
docstring
    Documentation for the generated class.

Returns
-------
str
    String representation of named class.
